text: Free markets allocate resources more efficiently than governments ever could.
Most state-owned enterprises should be sold off to private owners.
Businesses create prosperity best when regulation is kept light.
Price controls usually do more harm than good.
Cutting business red tape unleashes economic growth.
The state should own and operate major industries like energy and rail.
Government should actively direct investment toward strategic sectors.
Essential utilities are too important to be left to private profit.
A planned economy can serve ordinary people better than the market.
Markets need firm government management to work fairly.
Welfare benefits should be minimal and strictly temporary.
People have grown far too dependent on government handouts.
Able-bodied adults should work rather than collect benefits.
Generous welfare discourages people from finding jobs.
Charity and family, not the state, should catch those who fall.
Everyone deserves a generous government safety net.
The state should guarantee every citizen a basic income.
No one should go without healthcare because they cannot pay.
A strong welfare state is the mark of a decent society.
Unemployment benefits should be enough to live on with dignity.
We should impose tariffs to protect our domestic industries.
Trade deals have hollowed out our manufacturing heartland.
Buying domestic should be prioritized over cheap foreign imports.
Strategic industries deserve protection from foreign competition.
Globalized trade has cost ordinary workers their livelihoods.
Free trade between nations benefits everyone in the long run.
Tariffs mostly just raise prices for ordinary consumers.
We should tear down barriers to international commerce.
Open markets bring cheaper goods and wider choice.
Protectionism breeds inefficiency and invites retaliation.
Traditional family values are the foundation of a healthy society.
We have abandoned too many of our cultural traditions.
Society is strongest when it honors long-standing customs.
Recent changes to social norms have gone much too far.
Marriage and family should be understood in their traditional form.
Society should embrace evolving norms around gender and identity.
Progress means letting go of outdated social conventions.
Diverse family structures deserve equal respect and recognition.
Cultural change is healthy and should be welcomed.
People should be free to define their identity on their own terms.
Religious values should help guide our public policy.
Faith has a rightful place in our public institutions.
Schools should make room for prayer and religious observance.
Our laws should reflect our nation's religious heritage.
Political leaders should be guided by their faith.
Government should be strictly secular and neutral on religion.
Religion should be kept entirely out of lawmaking.
Public life is better when it stays free of religious influence.
No religion should receive special standing from the state.
Personal faith should have no bearing on public policy.
The government needs strong powers to maintain order.
Individual freedoms must sometimes yield to social stability.
A firm hand from the state keeps society from unraveling.
Order and security matter more than personal autonomy.
Citizens have a duty to defer to lawful authority.
Individual liberty should almost always outweigh state power.
People should be free to live as they choose without interference.
The state has no business dictating how we run our lives.
Personal autonomy is more important than social conformity.
Government should stay out of citizens' private choices.
The state should monitor communications to prevent terrorism.
Surveillance cameras help make our streets safer.
Security agencies need broad powers to keep us safe.
Giving up some privacy is worth greater security.
Authorities should be able to access encrypted data when needed.
Mass surveillance is a serious threat to a free society.
People have a right to privacy that the state must not breach.
Strong encryption should be protected, not weakened.
Security must never come at the cost of our civil liberties.
Governments spy on their own citizens far more than they should.
Hate speech should be banned by law.
Some ideas are too harmful to be given a public platform.
Government should regulate dangerous misinformation.
Society has a duty to silence open bigotry.
Deeply offensive expression can be legitimately restricted.
Even offensive speech deserves legal protection.
The answer to bad speech is more speech, not censorship.
Free expression must include ideas we find repugnant.
No one has a right not to be offended.
Governments should never decide which opinions are allowed.
Criminals deserve harsh punishment to deter crime.
Longer prison sentences make society safer.
Justice means making offenders pay for what they did.
We are far too soft on crime.
Some crimes warrant severe, exemplary punishment.
Prisons should focus on rehabilitation, not punishment.
We should address the causes of crime, not just lock people up.
Most offenders can be reformed and reintegrated into society.
Long sentences do little to make communities safer.
A justice system should aim to heal, not merely to punish.
Major decisions are best left to experts, not popular vote.
Too much direct democracy leads to reckless choices.
Some questions are too complex to put to a referendum.
Guardrails should limit what popular majorities can do.
A stable state needs checks on the passions of the crowd.
Ordinary citizens should decide big questions through referendums.
Power should rest directly with the people, not with elites.
The public can be trusted to govern itself.
More decisions should be put directly to a popular vote.
Democracy means letting the majority have its way.
Our public institutions generally serve the common good.
We should trust the judgment of experts and official agencies.
The civil service does its job honestly and competently.
Most public officials are genuinely trying to do the right thing.
Institutions like the courts deserve our confidence.
Government institutions cannot be trusted to tell the truth.
The establishment mainly serves its own interests.
Official experts are too often wrong or compromised.
Public institutions have lost any right to our trust.
Those in power routinely deceive the public.
Our system needs to be torn down and rebuilt from scratch.
Only radical change can fix what is truly broken.
Incremental tweaks will never deliver real justice.
We need bold, sweeping transformation, not cautious tinkering.
The status quo is beyond saving through gradual reform.
Change should come gradually, through careful reform.
Sweeping revolutions usually cause more harm than good.
Steady, practical improvement beats grand upheaval.
We should reform our institutions, not tear them down.
Lasting progress is built one step at a time.
The national government should set uniform policy across the country.
Central government is best placed to solve our biggest problems.
A strong central state prevents a patchwork of unequal rules.
National standards should override local variation.
Power is better concentrated at the national level.
Most decisions should be made by local and regional governments.
Power should be devolved as close to communities as possible.
Local authorities understand their people's needs best.
Central government should hand more power back to the regions.
Communities, not distant capitals, should run their own affairs.
We should put our own nation's interests first.
National sovereignty matters more than global cooperation.
Patriotism and national pride hold a country together.
Our first duty is to our own citizens, not to the world.
We should reclaim powers we have handed to international bodies.
We are global citizens with obligations beyond our borders.
International cooperation should take priority over national pride.
National borders matter less than our shared humanity.
We should pool sovereignty to solve global problems together.
Narrow nationalism is a real danger in the modern world.
Immigration levels should be significantly reduced.
Our borders should be tightly controlled.
Newcomers should be expected to fully assimilate.
We admit far more immigrants than we can absorb.
Illegal border crossings should be firmly stopped.
We should welcome immigrants and make it easier to come.
Open immigration enriches our society and our economy.
We have a duty to offer refuge to those fleeing danger.
Immigrants strengthen the communities they join.
Paths to citizenship should be broad and accessible.
Our military should intervene to stop atrocities abroad.
A strong nation must be willing to project power overseas.
We should maintain a large, active military presence worldwide.
Sometimes force is the only way to defend our values abroad.
We must confront hostile regimes before they can threaten us.
We should avoid foreign wars and focus on problems at home.
Military intervention abroad usually backfires.
We should bring our troops home and stay out of others' conflicts.
Diplomacy, not force, should drive our foreign policy.
Endless overseas commitments drain us and endanger us.
Technological progress will solve most of our problems.
We should embrace new technologies like AI quickly.
Innovation should be allowed to move fast, not held back.
The benefits of new technology outweigh the risks.
Automation and AI will make life better for almost everyone.
We should be cautious and slow with powerful new technologies.
New technology often causes harm before we understand it.
Powerful innovations like AI need strict oversight.
We should not deploy technologies we cannot fully control.
Rapid technological change threatens jobs and communities.
Economic growth should take priority over environmental rules.
We cannot sacrifice jobs for strict green regulations.
Environmental fears are too often used to hold back prosperity.
Cheap, reliable energy matters more than emissions targets.
Development should not be blocked by every environmental concern.
Protecting the environment matters more than economic growth.
We must cut emissions even if it slows the economy.
Nature has a value that no economic gain can justify destroying.
We should transition off fossil fuels as fast as possible.
Future generations depend on the environmental choices we make now.
Environmental rules should never be allowed to strangle free enterprise.
Government should regulate markets firmly to protect the planet.
A smaller state means lower taxes and leaner welfare, and that is for the best.
The government should run key services and guarantee people's basic needs.
Our traditions are rooted in faith, and both deserve public respect.
A modern society should be both secular and socially progressive.
Mass immigration erodes our shared national culture.
An open society is culturally richer for the immigrants it welcomes.
Putting our nation first means controlling who is allowed in.
Openness to the world means welcoming migrants, not shutting them out.
We should protect our own industries and our own nation first.
Global free trade and global cooperation go hand in hand.
A great nation must be willing to project strength on the world stage.
We should be humble abroad and cooperate rather than dominate.
The state should have broad powers to watch and police its citizens.
Freedom means limiting both state power and state surveillance.
Order requires strict laws and tough punishment for offenders.
A free society treats even wrongdoers with restraint and dignity.
The state may restrict what people say to preserve public order.
Liberty means the state cannot police what we say.
Authorities should be able to remove online content that threatens security.
Neither our privacy nor our speech should be surrendered to the state.
Insults and blasphemy against religion should not be permitted.
People must be free to mock or criticize any religion.
We should trust our institutions and let qualified experts govern.
Power belongs with ordinary people, not with a distrusted elite.
Sweeping change should wait for careful, guarded deliberation.
The people should rise up and demand radical change now.
Our corrupt institutions must be swept away entirely.
We should patiently reform the institutions we can still trust.
A strong central national government keeps the country united.
Power should flow down to local communities and outward to the world.
National leaders should set binding policy for everyone.
Local communities should directly govern their own affairs.
We must fight threats abroad and surveil dangers at home.
We should end foreign wars and stop spying on our own people.
Technology and industry should drive growth without heavy green limits.
We should slow down risky technology and put the planet first.
Automation will grow the economy and shrink the need for welfare.
As machines take jobs, the state must expand support for workers.
Governments should tightly regulate powerful new technologies.
Innovators should be free from heavy-handed government control.
Newcomers place too heavy a strain on public services and welfare.
We should extend the welfare state to support new arrivals.
Restoring traditional discipline means getting tough on crime.
A progressive society should rehabilitate offenders, not punish them.
We should not wreck our own economy for global climate agreements.
Climate change demands that all nations act together across borders.
Open, deregulated markets and free trade lift everyone.
The state should shield strategic industries with tariffs and subsidies.
The media spread misinformation and should be reined in.
A free press we can trust is essential to democracy.
The state should uphold and enforce religious moral standards.
People should be free from the authority of both church and state.
Dangerous populist rhetoric should sometimes be restricted.
The people must be free to speak their minds and to rule themselves.
The national government should guarantee the same welfare in every region.
Local communities, not the central state, should run their own welfare.
We need a revolution to overturn capitalism itself.
Gradual, market-friendly reform is the responsible path forward.
We should trust our security agencies to act firmly abroad.
Foreign adventures are driven by elites we have no reason to trust.
We should conserve our heritage and change it only slowly.
We need radical cultural transformation, and we need it soon.
We should intervene at the source to stop waves of migration.
We should welcome refugees rather than bomb the countries they flee.
We should trust intelligence agencies with strong surveillance powers.
Spy agencies abuse both our trust and our privacy.
Immigration is eroding our religious heritage.
A secular nation should welcome people of every faith and none.
